Because they just did it. You know they will, because they just did it. The likelihood of getting them to do that again, versus getting somebody who's never done it to do it, much easier to get the fifth one out of the person who can and will, than the first one out of the person who maybe they can't, or maybe they won't. So you make a big deal out of it, you get more. Measurement and management, this whole thing has to be measured in management. How are we doing? How many new patients, new customers, new clients this month? How many referrals from them, from this group? The first month? How many did we get the second month? How many did we get the third month? Next month's group? How many? Who has, who hasn't? Who hasn't for six months? We gotta do something about this. They're not carrying their weight. Staff, if the staff won't refer from their circle of influence, you have no hope of them motivating customers, clients, or patients to refer. So you gotta, the staff has to refer. Direct conversation with non-referring patient. At some trigger point, somebody has to sit with that patient, that customer, that client, say Dave, you know. You've been with me for seven months, you couldn't walk when you came in here, and now you're back on the trapeze. And our average patient has brought us three patients by now. What's wrong? If it's something, you know, somebody needs to have that conversation. And then, if the customer, client, patient says, no, no, I'm telling everybody I know about you, then you know what he's telling them is not helpful. So we gotta go back to tools. Well, it would be better if you just, you know. Forced referrals, so there's a whole forced referrals thing that we do not have time for, because the clock says five minutes. But it's in here. Yeah, it's all in here. Literally, you don't have to get anybody to refer. You could sort of create activity around them that forces it. And then there's optional stuff. There's the standardized rewards program that is in place continually, and you accumulate points, and you get stuff. There's the events, which we did talk about, and there's classes and seminars that we did talk about. Wow, fantastic. Wow. So again, what you need to do is you need to listen to this again, watch this again with no pad in hand. Make sure that you go through your manual, all right? Then create a list of things you're gonna start. Pick one to get started with, and get implemented. Then pick another one, and then just go down the line. And before you know it, you'll have your referral system in place, you'll have your machine in place in your business. But again, it's really important, as you said, that you manage and you monitor the results that you're getting, and you're constantly, you're training not only your customers, your clients, and patients, but your staff as well. Staff as well. So make sure you take care of that, and make sure you do that, because if you put this in place, you'll have more business coming in. You'll have more of your ideal customers, your clients, and patients coming in as well.
